summary:
There are currently FDA-accelerated approvals for treatments of METABOLIC DYSFUNCTION-ASSOCIATED STEATOTIC LIVER DISEASE (MASLD), including resmetirom and semaglutide, primarily for patients with moderate-to-advanced fibrosis. As MASLD encompassed previously defined NAFLD cases, especially those involving metabolic risk factors, these approvals constitute recognized therapeutic interventions for this condition despite being limited to specific subgroups. 

Note: This summary focuses only on clinically relevant facts related directly to the query regarding approved treatments for NAFLD/MASLD.
